arts.ttl: Team news: Wigan Athletic boss names side for Reading visit
arts.sum: Wigan Athletic manager Shaun Maloney has named an unchanged side for Saturday’s visit of Reading.

arts.ttl: Reading team news: Andy Yiadom makes first squad in 320 days for Wigan Athletic trip
arts.sum: Reading captain Andy Yiadom has returned to the matchday squad for the first time in 320 days at Wigan Athletic. The 33-year-old limped off during a 2-0 win over Bristol Rovers in April but has featured in the last three Under-21 fixtures, including a 3-0 defeat to West Ham on Monday. Fit enough for a place on the bench at the Brick Community Stadium, Noel Hunt has made three changes for the visit to Lancashire. Joel Pereia remains between the posts, hoping for a third clean sheet in four matches, while Michael Craig is replaced at right-back by youngster Tivonge Rushesha. Tyler Bindon and Amadou Mbengue remain the central defensive pairing and Andre Garcia keeps his place at left-back.

arts.ttl: Live updates: Reading aim to keep play-off push alive with Wigan Athletic test
arts.sum: Reading look to register a sixth match unbeaten and keep up their push for the play-offs at Wigan Athletic this afternoon. Three points outside the top six heading into the final 14 matches, the Royals are in touching distance. However, the Latics are three matches unbeaten themselves, winning against Huddersfield Town on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Wigan Athletic: Our expected team v Reading!
arts.sum: Paul Kendrick runs the rule over the Wigan Athletic squad and predicts the 11 men who Shaun Maloney will select to start against Reading at the Brick Community Stadium on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Wigan Athletic boss 'hopes' Reading get 'the right owner' ahead of meeting
arts.sum: Wigan Athletic boss Shaun Maloney has spoken briefly of the off-field crisis facing the club, hoping they find 'the right owner for them.' The former Scotland international has been in charge at the Brick Community Stadium since 2022 and faced Noel Hunt on the day which the Latics were relegated to League One in Berkshire, less than a week before the Royals followed. Having been through their own issues, Wigan saved by a local businessman in administration less than three years ago, they are a club and fanbase who know what the Royals are currently facing with Dai Yongge at the helm. However, there is a bit more positivity in Reading as Robert Platek moves into exclusive negotiations over a quick takeover, with the club now having been 'up for sale' for over 500 days.

arts.ttl: Reading captain available for League One return at Wigan Athletic after 10 months out
arts.sum: Reading captain Andy Yiadom is available to make his long-awaited first-team return tomorrow. The 33-year-old has not featured since April after suffering injury during a 2-0 win at Bristol Rovers. Since then, the club captain has worked his way back and featured in the last three Under-21 matches, incrementally increasing his load until lasting the full 90 minutes in Monday's 3-0 defeat to West Ham United. The Royals sit ninth in League One with 14 matches remaining, only three points outside the play-offs ahead of the trip to Lancashire.
